volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 4.3.945b - Section 4.3.1011b)

sections.label: Section 4.3.945b
sections.translation: Listen .

sections.label: Section 4.3.946
sections.translation: I won ’ t listen .

sections.label: Section 4.3.946b
sections.translation: But , upon my faith , you shall listen .

sections.label: Section 4.3.946c
sections.translation: Nay but , another time , tell me what you want .

sections.label: Section 4.3.947
sections.original: Eho máne dum , est óperae pretium quod tíbi ego narraré volo .
sections.translation: Come now , it ’ s worth your while at once to hear what I want to tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.948
sections.translation: Say on , whatever it is .

sections.label: Section 4.3.948b
sections.original: Víde num quispíam consequitur própe nos .
sections.translation: See whether any person is following near us .

sections.label: Section 4.3.949
sections.translation: Why , what reason is there that it should matter to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.949b
sections.translation: So it is ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.950
sections.original: séd boni cónsili écquid in té mihi est ?
sections.translation: but can you give me some good advice ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.951
sections.translation: What ’ s the business ? Only tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.951b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; keep silence ; if only you ’ ll give me your word that you won ’ t prove treacherous to me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.953
sections.original: Dó fidem tibi , fídus ero , quísquis es .
sections.translation: I do give you my word ; I ’ ll be true to you , whoever you are .

sections.label: Section 4.3.953b
sections.translation: Listen . I saw a person commit a theft ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.955
sections.translation: I knew the owner to whom that same property belonged . Afterwards I came myself to the thief , and I made him a proposal in these terms : I know the person on whom that theft was committed ; now if you are ready to give me half , I ’ ll not make a discovery to the owner .

sections.label: Section 4.3.960
sections.original: is míhi nihil etiam réspondit . quid inde aéquom est dari mihi ? dímidium
sections.translation: He didn ’ t even give me an answer . What is it fair should be given me out of it ? Half , I trust you will say .

sections.label: Section 4.3.961b
sections.original: Immo hercle étiam amplius , nam nísi dat , domino dícundum
sections.translation: Aye , even more ; but unless he gives it you , I think it ought to be told to the owner .

sections.label: Section 4.3.962b
sections.original: Tuo consilió faciam . nunc ádvorte animum ; námque hoc omne
sections.translation: I ’ ll act on your advice . Now give me your attention ; for it is to yourself all this relates .

sections.label: Section 4.3.962ab
sections.translation: What has been done by me ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.963
sections.original: Vídulum istum cúiust novi ego hóminem iam pridem .
sections.translation: I ’ ve known the person for a long time to whom that wallet belongs .

sections.label: Section 4.3.963b
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.964
sections.translation: And in what manner it was lost .

sections.label: Section 4.3.964b
sections.original: At ego quo pacto inventust scio
sections.translation: But I know in what manner it was found ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.965
sections.original: et qui invenit hominem novi et dominus qui nunc est scio .
sections.translation: and I know the person who found it , and who is now the owner . That , I ’ faith , is not a bit the more your matter than it is my own . I know the person to whom it now belongs ; you , the person to whom it formerly belonged . This shall no individual get away from me ; don ’ t you be expecting to get it in a hurry .

sections.label: Section 4.3.969
sections.translation: If the owner comes , shan ’ t he get it away ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.969b
sections.translation: That you mayn ’ t be mistaken , no born person is there that ’ s owner of this but my own self — who took this in my own fishing .

sections.label: Section 4.3.971
sections.translation: Was it really so ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.971b
sections.original: Ecquem esse dices in mari piscem meum ?
sections.translation: Which fish in the sea will you say is my own ? When I catch them , if indeed I do catch them , they are my own ; as my own I keep them . They are not claimed as having a right to freedom [*] nor does any person demand a share in them . In the market I sell them all openly as my own wares .

sections.label: Section 4.3.975
sections.translation: Indeed , the sea is , surely , common to all persons .

sections.label: Section 4.3.975b
sections.translation: I agree to that ; prithee , then , why any the less is it proper that this wallet should be common to me ? It was found in the sea .

sections.label: Section 4.3.977b
sections.translation: Assuredly you are an outrageously impudent fellow ; for if this is justice which you are saying , then fishermen would be ruined . Inasmuch as , the moment that the fish were exposed upon the stalls ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.980
sections.original: nemo emat , suam quisque partem piscium poscant sibi ,
sections.translation: no one would buy them ; every person would be demanding his own share of the fish for himself ; he would be saying that they were caught in the sea that was common to all .

sections.label: Section 4.3.981b
sections.translation: What do you say , you impudent fellow ? Do you dare to compare a wallet with fish ? Pray , does it appear to be the same thing ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.983b
sections.translation: The matter doesn ’ t lie in my power ; when I ’ ve cast my hook and net into the sea , whatever has adhered I draw out .

sections.label: Section 4.3.985
sections.original: meum quod rete atque hami nancti sunt , meum potissimumst .
sections.translation: Whatever my net and hooks have got , that in especial is my own .

sections.label: Section 4.3.986
sections.original: Immo hercle haud est , siquidem quod vas excepisti .
sections.translation: Nay but , I ’ faith , it is not ; if , indeed , you ’ ve fished up any article that ’ s made [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.986b
sections.translation: Philosopher , you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.987
sections.original: Sed tu enumquam piscatorem vidisti , venefice ,
sections.translation: But look now , you conjurer , did you ever see a fisherman who caught a wallet-fish , or exposed one for sale in the market ? But , indeed , you shan ’ t here be taking possession of all the profits that you choose ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.990
sections.original: et vitorem et piscatorem te esse , impure , postulas .
sections.translation: you expect , you dirty fellow , to be both a maker of wallets [*] and a fisherman . Either you must show me a fish that is a wallet , or else you shall carry nothing off that wasn ’ t produced in the sea and has no scales .

sections.label: Section 4.3.993
sections.original: Quid , tu numquam audisti esse antehac vidulum piscem ?
sections.translation: What , did you never hear before to-day that a wallet was a fish ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.993b
sections.translation: Villain , there is no such fish .

sections.label: Section 4.3.994b
sections.original: Immo est profecto ; ego , qui sum piscator , scio ;
sections.translation: Yes , there certainly is ; I , who am a fisherman , know it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.995
sections.original: verum raro capitur , nullus minus saepe ad terram venit .
sections.translation: But it is seldom caught ; no fish more rarely comes near the land .

sections.label: Section 4.3.996
sections.original: Nil agis , dare verba speras mihi te posse , furcifer .
sections.translation: It ’ s to no purpose ; you hope that you can be cheating me , you rogue . Of what colour is it ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.997
sections.original: Quo colore est , hoc colore capiuntur pauxilluli ;
sections.translation: Of this colour very few are caught : some are of a purple skin , there are great and black ones also .

sections.label: Section 4.3.998b
sections.translation: I understand ; by my troth , you ’ ll be turning into a wallet-fish I fancy , if you don ’ t take care ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.1000
sections.original: fiet tibi puniceum corium , postea atrum denuo .
sections.translation: your skin will be purple , and then afterwards black .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1001
sections.translation: What a villain this that I have met with to-day !

sections.label: Section 4.3.1001b
sections.translation: We are wasting words ; the day wears apace . Consider , please , by whose arbitration do you wish us to proceed ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1002b
sections.translation: By the arbitration of the wallet .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1003b
sections.translation: Really so , indeed ? You are a fool .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1003c
sections.translation: My respects to you , Mister Thales [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.1004
sections.original: Tu istunc hodie non feres , nisi das sequestrum aut arbitrum ,
sections.translation: You shan ’ t carry that off this day , unless you find a place of safe keeping for it , or an umpire ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.1005
sections.translation: by whose arbitration this matter may be settled .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1005b
sections.translation: Prithee , are you in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1006
sections.translation: I ’ m mad , in need of hellebore .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1006b
sections.original: At ego cerritus , hunc non amittam tamen .
sections.translation: But I ’ m troubled with sprites ; still I shan ’ t let this go .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1007
sections.original: Verbum etiam adde unum , iam in cerebro colaphos apstrudam tuo ;
sections.translation: Only add a single word more , that instant I ’ ll drive my fists smash into your brains . This instant on this spot , just as a new napkin is wont to be wrung , I ’ ll wring out of you whatever moisture there is , if you don ’ t let this go .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1010
sections.original: Tange : adfligam ad terram te itidem ut piscem soleo polypum .
sections.translation: Touch me ; I ’ ll dash you down on the ground just in such fashion as I ’ m in the habit of doing with a polypus fish [*] . Would you like to fight ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1011b
sections.original: Quid opust ? quin tu potius praedam divide .
sections.translation: What need is there ? Nay , in preference , divide the booty .
